join_order.notes: Bloom filter (12MB, fits LLC) pre-screens anti-join. ~99% of probed rows have matches in pre → Bloom says 'in set' → skip immediately with no index probe. ~1% have no match → Bloom says 'not in set' → aggregate directly. Only ~0.8% false positives (~720 rows) need full index probe. Reduces 9M random probes to ~720.

scan.access_pattern: sequential within qualifying blocks, two-phase: filter then bloom+probe+aggregate

data_structures.bloom_filter: Blocked Bloom filter for anti-join pre-screening. Parameters: n=9,600,799 pre entries, m=96M bits (12MB), k=7 hash functions. Build by scanning pre index entries and inserting each (sub_fk, tag_code, version_code) tuple. Use the same hashKey3 function as seed, then derive k bit positions via double-hashing: h1=lower32 of hash, h2=upper32, bit_i = (h1 + i*h2) % m. Store as uint64_t array of size m/64 = 1,500,000. During probe: if ALL k bits are set → 'maybe in set' (need full index probe only for anti-join survivors); if ANY bit unset → 'definitely not in set' (passes anti-join, aggregate directly). CRITICAL: Bloom positive means row likely HAS a pre match → SKIP it (fails anti-join). Bloom negative means row has NO match → passes anti-join → aggregate.
data_structures.uom_dict: load dictionary to find USD code
data_structures.pre_index: mmap pre_by_adsh_tag_ver.idx with MADV_RANDOM — only used for ~720 false-positive Bloom probes
data_structures.aggregation: Custom FlatHashMap struct with: (1) keys array of uint64_t initialized to 0 as sentinel via MAP_ANONYMOUS, (2) parallel vals array of AggState {int64_t cnt; double total;}, (3) occupied_slots vector<uint32_t> tracking indices of written slots. Size = 131072 slots (power of 2). Key encoding: ((tag_code<<32)|version_code)+1 to avoid sentinel collision. Linear probing with multiplicative hash. On insert of NEW key, push slot index to occupied_slots.
data_structures.selection_vector: thread-local uint32_t sel[100000] holding qualifying row indices within each block

parallelism.aggregation_strategy: Each thread owns one FlatHashMap with occupied_slots tracking. After scan, merge by iterating only occupied_slots of each thread-local map (total ~50K entries across all threads) instead of scanning all 131K×32 = 4.2M slots.
parallelism.bloom_build: Single-threaded build of Bloom filter during data_loading phase. 9.6M entries × 7 hash ops = ~67M bit-set operations, takes <5ms.

indexes.purpose: restrict scan to USD rows only
anti_join_probe for Bloom false positives only

optimization_notes: Category C (work elimination): Two root causes. (1) main_scan=28ms: 9M anti-join probes into the 153MB pre index (far exceeds 44MB LLC), but ~99% of probed rows have matches and are discarded. Fix: build a Bloom filter (12MB, fits LLC) from all pre (sub_fk, tag_code, version_code) tuples. Bloom negative = definitely no match = passes anti-join, aggregate directly. Bloom positive = likely has match = skip row immediately. Only ~0.8% false positives (~720 rows) need the expensive full index probe. This eliminates ~9M random cache-miss-heavy probes, replacing them with cache-resident Bloom checks. Expected main_scan reduction: 28ms → ~12-15ms. (2) merge_having_topk=8ms: merging 32 thread-local maps requires scanning 32×131K = 4.2M slots, but only ~50K groups exist. Fix: each FlatHashMap tracks occupied slot indices in a vector<uint32_t>. On new key insertion, push the slot index. Merge iterates only occupied slots (~50K total vs 4.2M). Expected merge reduction: 8ms → ~1-2ms. Total expected: ~45-50ms.
